top_categories.workout_context: 5,500ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Flandrien Ride's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
At mile 60 of 99, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
56ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Flandrien Ride. Train it until it feels boring.
Flandrien Ride's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 99 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,500ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 99 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 99 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
At Flandrien Ride, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mixed terrain. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Tempo is Flandrien Ride's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 99 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Flandrien Ride’s 99 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Flandrien Ride. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 99 miles of mixed terrain in Oudenaarde, Belgium.
